name: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act
owner: US Federal Trade Commission (Safeguards Rule); SEC; banking regulators
description: US federal law (Pub. L. 106-102, 1999) governing financial institutions' handling of customer information. Three rules: (1) Financial Privacy Rule (15 USC §§6801-6809) requires notice of information-sharing practices and opt-out rights; (2) Safeguards Rule (16 CFR Part 314) requires written information security programs; (3) Pretexting Provisions criminalize obtaining customer financial information under false pretenses.
relationToCloudSecurity: Financial institutions using cloud services must include cloud providers in their GLBA Safeguards Rule scope. FTC's 2021 amendments to the Safeguards Rule explicitly require security risk assessments covering third-party service providers.
relationToAISecurity: AI systems used by financial institutions for customer-data processing fall under Safeguards Rule. Banking regulator guidance (OCC, FDIC, Fed) increasingly addresses AI-specific risks within the GLBA framework.

license.notes: US federal statutes are public-domain government works (17 USC §105) and may be freely redistributed.

lifecycle.notes: Safeguards Rule materially updated by FTC in 2021 (effective 2023) — added written program requirements, encryption, MFA, and incident response obligations.

current_extraction.notes: Parsed from marker-extracted PLAW markdown via extract_us_plaw.py. Found 147 SEC./§ markers. Count includes some Table-of-Contents entries (US Public Laws inline-list section names in their preamble before the body); over-coverage rather than under.

extraction_history.reason: Basic PLAW section extraction from marker markdown

notes: Foundational US financial-services privacy/security law. Acquire 16 CFR Part 314 from eCFR for structured extraction.
